strengths: Strong SQL and Python ETL experience
Proven dashboarding with Power BI and Tableau
Experience integrating data from APIs and databases
Quantified impact metrics across roles and projects
Familiarity with cloud data platforms (Snowflake, BigQuery, AWS S3)
weaknesses: Only ~2 years total relevant experience in analytics roles
No explicit senior stakeholder presentation examples in corporate media context
improvement_bullets: Developed and maintained interactive dashboards in Power BI and Looker Studio to provide cross-functional teams with real-time insights into partnership performance and revenue KPIs.
Integrated data from third-party APIs (including commerce and syndication platforms) using Python to build unified performance views that support business development and partnership strategy.
Presented complex analytical findings and forecast models to senior leadership and cross-departmental stakeholders in Business Development and Product to drive actionable decision-making.
Collaborated with cross-functional stakeholders to define data governance standards and documentation processes, ensuring high data quality for reporting and BI initiatives.
Applied advanced data modeling and segmentation techniques to audience and content-level datasets, directly informing content prioritization and growth initiatives.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Analyst, Business Intelligence opportunity with Hearst Magazines. I am interested in this opportunity because Hearst’s portfolio of iconic brands—from Cosmopolitan to Popular Mechanics—represents the intersection of high-quality content and cutting-edge data, which is exactly the environment where I aim to grow my career. What interests me about working for Hearst Magazines is its commitment to using data to fuel audience engagement and partnership strategy, which inspires me as a data professional who believes in the power of evidence-based storytelling. I believe my proficiency in Python-driven ETL pipelines and my expertise in data visualization would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by focusing on the translation of raw data into executive-level strategy. In fact, at Shine Masters, I identified a bottleneck in our operational reporting and replaced manual processes with automated Power BI dashboards. The result was a 40% reduction in data processing time and a 300% increase in customer acquisition through targeted, data-backed marketing initiatives. Ultimately, I know that the Analyst, Business Intelligence role at Hearst Magazines would allow me to bring my experience with API integration and ETL development to the Business Development and Partnerships team. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst at Shine Masters enabled me to develop my stakeholder communication skills. I managed the end-to-end process of translating complex operational performance data into clear, actionable insights for leadership, effectively aligning technical reporting with business objectives. I am excited by the opportunity to use my background in Python and SQL to assist in analyzing revenue and content-level datasets at Hearst. I know that my technical versatility and my focus on delivering impactful, clear insights align with what it takes to succeed at the Analyst, Business Intelligence role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
